text:
“Go abroad if need be,” Christophe Dubi, the I.O.C. official responsible for the Games, said in an interview during the Olympics in Milan, referring to the possibility of holding the sliding events elsewhere. The Italian hosts, he noted fought to keep everything at home. “From an I.O.C. standpoint, we had the door wide open, including when we said the timeline was incredibly tight. We said, ‘Listen, we still have this option — we can use something outside.’”
The I.O.C. has had to pivot toward flexibility as the number of bidders for its marquee winter event has started to dwindle, amid criticism about sustainability, changing weather and the economic and infrastructure demands it places on host cities. For the last edition, in 2022, it was left with just two choices: Almaty, Kazakhstan, and its eventual choice, Beijing.

title: Some Olympic Leaders Want to See Fixed Winter Games Host Cities
